what_it_does: Renders a small animated chip when a relative centre is active (centre is non-null), showing the current centre label and a plain-language reset affordance, and calls onReset when clicked to return to the root origin.
observations: Uses AnimatePresence and motion from framer-motion for enter/exit animation.
Conditionally renders only when centre is truthy.
Props are centre: Centre | null and onReset: () => void.
Uses stamp('ui://controls/centre') and transition('enter') from project utilities.
Visible text reads 'Centred on {label} show all ×'.
Provides title and aria-label sentences that describe both state and action.
Uses CSS classes: centrechip, centrechip-glyph, centrechip-pre, centrechip-label, centrechip-x.
standouts: Strong accessibility: both title and aria-label explicitly state the current state and the reset action.
Plain-language reset affordance 'show all ×' instead of only an icon.
Motion wrapper gives the chip an enter/exit transition.
conventions: React functional component with typed props.
Imports types from sibling modules and tokens from a shared motion token file.
Hides decorative glyph from assistive tech with aria-hidden.
concerns: Title and aria-label text are nearly identical, which may be redundant for screen-reader users.
The visible 'show all ×' phrasing could be read as a label rather than a button action.
No visible focus or active-state handling in the file itself; styling is external.
onReset is passed in but not validated or guarded.
notes: A small, accessible state chip that tells the operator what the view is centred on and offers a clear, human-language way back to the root.
questions: What other fields does the Centre type contain besides label?
Does stamp return props that are safe to spread onto a motion.button?
Are the CSS classes and focus styles defined in a stylesheet elsewhere?
Is the visible text intended to be localized?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it shows the active relative centre and lets the operator reset to root when centre is non-null.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the current projection origin visible and to give users an obvious, human-language path back to the root origin after they have changed the view centre.
contribution: Provides a focused, accessible, animated indicator for an active projection centre with a plain-language reset action, useful as a reusable pattern for state disclosure and recovery.
summary_for_embedding: CentreChip is a React component that conditionally renders an animated chip showing the active relative centre label and a 'show all' reset button, with accessible labels and motion transitions.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to surface the current view centre and give users a clear, plain-language way to return to the root origin.

symbols.does: Renders an animated reset chip when a non-root centre is active.
symbols.description: CentreChip is a React functional component that receives an optional Centre and an onReset callback. When centre is present, it renders a motion.button with enter/exit animations, an address stamp, visible text that reads 'Centred on {label}', a 'show all ×' reset affordance, and accessible title/aria-label text that explains both the current state and the reset action.
